question: What is the refund policy for annual subscriptions?
ground_truth: Annual subscriptions can be refunded within 30 days of purchase. After 30 days, refunds are prorated based on remaining months. No refunds are given after 6 months.
contexts: Our refund policy states that annual subscriptions are eligible for a full refund within 30 days of the initial purchase date.
After the 30-day window, customers may request a prorated refund calculated based on the number of unused months remaining.
Please note that no refunds will be issued for annual subscriptions after 6 months from the purchase date.
generated_answer: Annual subscriptions can be fully refunded within 30 days. After that, you can get a prorated refund based on unused months. No refunds are available after 6 months.

question: How do I reset my password?
ground_truth: To reset your password, click 'Forgot Password' on the login page, enter your email, and follow the link sent to your inbox. The link expires in 24 hours.
contexts: To reset your password: 1) Go to the login page, 2) Click 'Forgot Password', 3) Enter your registered email address, 4) Check your inbox for the reset link.
Password reset links are valid for 24 hours. If the link expires, you'll need to request a new one.
generated_answer: Click 'Forgot Password' on the login page, enter your email, and check your inbox for a reset link. The link is valid for 24 hours.

question: What programming languages does the API support?
ground_truth: The API has official SDKs for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Java. Community SDKs exist for Ruby, PHP, and C#.
contexts: We provide official SDK support for Python, JavaScript (including TypeScript), Go, and Java.
Community-maintained SDKs are available for Ruby, PHP, and C#. These are not officially supported but are regularly tested against our API.
generated_answer: The API officially supports Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, and Java. There are also community SDKs for Ruby, PHP, and C#.

question: What is the maximum file upload size?
ground_truth: The maximum file upload size is 100MB for standard plans and 500MB for enterprise plans. Files larger than 10MB are processed asynchronously.
contexts: File upload limits: Standard plan - 100MB maximum per file. Enterprise plan - 500MB maximum per file.
Files exceeding 10MB in size will be processed asynchronously. You'll receive a webhook notification when processing is complete.
generated_answer: The max upload size is 100MB for standard plans and 500MB for enterprise. Files over 10MB are processed asynchronously.

question: How does the billing cycle work?
ground_truth: Billing occurs on the first of each month. You're charged based on usage from the previous month. Invoices are sent via email 3 days before the charge.
contexts: All accounts are billed on the 1st of each month for the previous month's usage.
Invoices are generated and emailed 3 business days prior to the billing date, giving you time to review charges.
generated_answer: You're billed on the 1st of each month for the previous month's usage. Invoices are emailed 3 days before the charge date.

question: Can I use the service in the EU?
ground_truth: Yes, we have EU data centers in Frankfurt and Amsterdam. EU customers' data is stored exclusively in EU regions to comply with GDPR.
contexts: We operate data centers in Frankfurt (Germany) and Amsterdam (Netherlands) for our European customers.
All data for EU-based customers is stored exclusively within EU regions, ensuring full GDPR compliance.
Our service is available globally with regional data residency options.
generated_answer: Yes, we have EU data centers in Frankfurt and Amsterdam. Your data stays in the EU for GDPR compliance.

question: What authentication methods are supported?
ground_truth: We support API keys, OAuth 2.0, and SAML SSO. API keys are recommended for server-to-server communication, OAuth for user-facing apps, and SAML for enterprise SSO.
contexts: Supported authentication methods: API Keys, OAuth 2.0, and SAML-based Single Sign-On (SSO).
API keys are best for server-to-server integrations. OAuth 2.0 is recommended for applications acting on behalf of users. SAML SSO is available for enterprise customers.
generated_answer: We support API keys (for server-to-server), OAuth 2.0 (for user-facing apps), and SAML SSO (for enterprise). API keys are the simplest option for backend integrations.

question: What happens when I exceed my rate limit?
ground_truth: When you exceed the rate limit, you receive a 429 status code. Requests are queued for up to 60 seconds. After that, they're rejected. Rate limits reset every minute.
contexts: Exceeding rate limits returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests). Your requests will be automatically queued for up to 60 seconds.
If the queue is full or 60 seconds pass without capacity, the request is rejected with a 429 error and Retry-After header.
Rate limits are calculated on a per-minute rolling window basis.
generated_answer: You'll get a 429 error. Requests are queued for up to 60 seconds, then rejected if capacity isn't available. Limits reset per minute.

question: How do I migrate from v1 to v2 of the API?
ground_truth: The migration guide covers 3 breaking changes: the auth header changed from X-API-Key to Authorization Bearer, response format changed from XML to JSON, and pagination switched from offset to cursor-based.
contexts: V2 Migration Breaking Changes: 1) Authentication header changed from 'X-API-Key: <key>' to 'Authorization: Bearer <key>'.
2) All response formats are now JSON (XML responses deprecated). 3) Pagination migrated from offset-based to cursor-based for better performance.
A migration helper tool is available at /tools/migrate-v1-to-v2 that can automatically update your code.
generated_answer: Three breaking changes in v2: auth header is now 'Authorization: Bearer', responses are JSON only (no more XML), and pagination is cursor-based instead of offset. There's a migration tool available to help.

question: What SLA do you guarantee?
ground_truth: We guarantee 99.9% uptime for standard plans and 99.99% for enterprise. Downtime credits are 10x the cost of the downtime period. Scheduled maintenance windows are excluded.
contexts: Service Level Agreement: Standard plans - 99.9% uptime guarantee. Enterprise plans - 99.99% uptime guarantee.
If we fail to meet the SLA, affected customers receive credits worth 10x the cost of the downtime period.
Scheduled maintenance windows (announced 72 hours in advance) are excluded from SLA calculations.
generated_answer: Standard plans get 99.9% uptime, enterprise gets 99.99%. If we miss the SLA, you get 10x credits for the downtime period. Scheduled maintenance doesn't count against the SLA.
